video_title: The Mahindra XUV 7XO Gets (almost) Everything Right

features.name: design team
verbatim: there are two design teams within Mahindra…..one team led by dada and the other one full of interns designing individual parts in isolation and a production guy ensuring how to put it all together

features.name: interior color
verbatim: their interior colour scheme is also not convoluted
endless beige/white interiors
not a big fan of the cream interiors.. glad i dont have money to buy this, one less worry

features.name: physical controls
verbatim: missing physical buttons for AC controls
the lower varients have xuv 700 steering and hivac buttons unlike the top spec
the absence of physical dials are such an emotional disconnect
All physical touch controls with that 2L turbo petrol engine.
they moved the buttons so that we will use the adrenox + alexa for these

features.name: steering wheel
verbatim: the ugly steering

features.name: touch buttons
verbatim: the touch buttons are the biggest gripe with what seems to be a phenomenal product
Lower models still have physical controls. It's only the top end models that have touch controls.
Please fix the capacitive buttons, this can be really deal breaker if it isn’t working half of the times and if possible include HVAC controls.
a row of touch sensitive buttons which aren't really touch sensitive, they have some haptic thing going on and sometimes they work and sometimes they don't

features.name: exterior design
verbatim: I think Mahindra should fire pratap bose for shitty design

features.name: facelift
verbatim: Is this a Marazzo facelift?

features.name: interior color
verbatim: the tan option in the Thar Roxx would've been better than the jigsaw puzzle they've ended up with

features.name: buttonless interior
verbatim: the button less front cabin looks aweful !!!! Also very impractical

features.name: climate controls
verbatim: A/C hivac controls integrated in the screen

features.name: depreciation
verbatim: tech-heavy cars can depreciate faster

verbatim: endless beige/white interiors

verbatim: Mahindra could have given it power tailgate. Its a shame thay they r not offering it while its available on rivals

verbatim: the ugly steering

features.name: touch buttons
verbatim: the touch interface is a deal breaker

features.name: exterior design
verbatim: the XUV700 to me honestly was one of the best looking Mahindras. It looked confident, it looked restrained and honestly looked like a world-class European SUV.

features.name: front design
verbatim: Even front design they have spoiled

features.name: infotainment screens
verbatim: they seriously need to ditch that many Screen. You basically need just one which is good for navigation and a little entrainment if needed.

features.name: interior color
verbatim: It looks like some car sickness guy went to Ladakh in that and forget to clean it after the multiple vomits!

features.name: interior design
verbatim: Have the interiors been designed in consultation with Karol Bagh experts?

features.name: physical controls
verbatim: It loses out on looks, physical buttons and frankly interiors overall.

features.name: model comparison
verbatim: Xuv500 better

features.name: interior color
verbatim: mahindra will change it if everyone hates it , just like how they did it in the ROXX

features.name: blind spot detection
verbatim: the blind spot monitor view in the instrument cluster was good, but it had a very slow frame rate and that is fixed

features.name: boss mode feature
verbatim: you can also control the front seat from back here via the electric boss mode

features.name: climate controls
verbatim: Mahindra has taken away the air conditioning controls and given you a touch sensitive panel here instead
the express cool button on the key fob that allows you to kickstart the air conditioning on your car a minute or two before you get inside

simple things like climate control is a, like, sure, there's a button here for the fan, but if that button wasn't there, it's a three-step process to go into the screen and control the climate control, and even then, the menu for the climate control is just, it's very confusing

features.name: device mount
verbatim: you can also, if you buy the right mount, place your iPhone or iPad here magnetically and then charge it via a type C port with a 65 watt output

features.name: drive mode controls
verbatim: Now if I press this, it does change the drive modes now

features.name: front design
verbatim: now, the entire front end looks out of place, at least to me

features.name: front passenger seat
verbatim: the front passenger seat was manual, that's fixed. This one is electric

features.name: infotainment responsiveness
verbatim: because of the faster processor, they are very responsive and to be honest, we faced zero issues with them on our drive

features.name: infotainment screens
verbatim: the infotainment screen still has fairly large bezels
it's a triple screen layout and these are very high resolution screens
These screens also get Dolby Vision for better contrast and colours

features.name: logo placement
verbatim: I'm happy to report that they have fixed that logo at the back here

features.name: rear seat ventilation
verbatim: There's also rear seat ventilation

features.name: second row seat
verbatim: the second row didn't slide and it still doesn't slide

features.name: sunshades
verbatim: there's sunshades

features.name: third row space
verbatim: the third row was cramped and because this is a facelift, it is still cramped

features.name: touch buttons
verbatim: a row of touch sensitive buttons which aren't really touch sensitive, they have some haptic thing going on and sometimes they work and sometimes they don't
I know I ranted a lot about the touch sensitive controls, I think I said it three times or four times in the video and it might sound very negative, but I'll tell you what the problem is

features.name: wireless charging
verbatim: you get a wireless charging pad with a cooling function

features.name: ADAS features
verbatim: an even better ADAS system

features.name: airbags
verbatim: They've also given it six airbags as standard with the curtain airbag extending till the third row.

features.name: cabin noise
verbatim: Mahindra has made the cabin quieter

features.name: engine power
verbatim: powerful engines

features.name: exterior design
verbatim: This is shouting for attention, it's too busy, there's just too much going on everywhere you look and I'm sorry, but I feel it's a step back in terms of design.

features.name: feature list
verbatim: more features than you would ever need

features.name: frameless IRVM
verbatim: a beautiful frameless IRVM

features.name: interior space
verbatim: ample space on the inside

features.name: pricing
verbatim: the price is right as well. It starts at 13.6 lakh rupees, which is exactly the same as what the 700 used to start at, but now with a lot more equipment.

features.name: ride quality
verbatim: it's got great ride quality

features.name: seat cushioning
verbatim: they've even improved the cushioning of the seats

verbatim: There's a better sound system

features.name: ride quality
verbatim: I really think it's come close to taking the crown away from the Harrier and Safari for the best ride and handling package in the segment

verbatim: the new Kia has brought back the tactile buttons
Hyundai, Kia, Volkswagen, all the big guys have moved back to nice physical controls for functions that are important and critical and need to be used every single day
